tool.name: Metabolic Reaction Enrichment Analysis
tool.command:
python $__tool_directory__/marea.py
--tool_dir $__tool_directory__ --out_log $log
#if $cond.type_selector != 'user_pvalues': --option $cond.type_selector #end if
#if $cond.type_selector == 'datasets':
--using_RAS $cond.using_ras.check --using_RPS $cond.using_rps.check
#if $cond.using_ras.check == 'true': --input_datas #for $data in $cond.using_ras.input_datasets: ${data.input} #end for
--names #for $data in $cond.using_ras.input_datasets: ${data.input_name} #end for #end if
#if $cond.using_rps.check == 'true': --input_datas_rps #for $data in $cond.using_rps.input_datasets_rps: ${data.input_rps} #end for
--names_rps #for $data in $cond.using_rps.input_datasets_rps: ${data.input_name_rps} #end for #end if
--net ${cond.using_net_rps.check} #if $cond.using_net_rps.check == 'true': --net_rps_input ${cond.using_net_rps.net_rps_file} #end if
--comparison ${cond.comparis.comparison} #if $cond.comparis.comparison == 'onevsmany' --control '${cond.comparis.controlgroup}' #end if
#if $cond.advanced.choice == 'true': --test ${cond.advanced.test} --pValue ${cond.advanced.pValue} --adjusted ${cond.advanced.adjusted} --fChange ${cond.advanced.fChange} --generate_svg ${cond.advanced.generateSvg} --generate_pdf ${cond.advanced.generatePdf} --upRegCol '#${cond.advanced.upRegCol}' --downRegCol '#${cond.advanced.downRegCol}' #else --test 'ks' --pValue 0.05 --adjusted false --fChange 1.2 --generate_svg false --generate_pdf true --upRegCol '#ecac68' --downRegCol '#6495ed' #end if
#elif $cond.type_selector == 'dataset_class':
--using_RAS $cond.using_ras_all.check --using_RPS $cond.using_rps_all.check
#if $cond.using_ras_all.check == 'true': --input_data ${cond.using_ras_all.input_data} --input_class ${cond.using_ras_all.input_class} #end if
#if $cond.using_rps_all.check == 'true': --input_data_rps ${cond.using_rps_all.input_data_rps} --input_class_rps ${cond.using_rps_all.input_class_rps} #end if
--net ${cond.using_net_rps.check} #if $cond.using_net_rps.check == 'true': --net_rps_input ${cond.using_net_rps.net_rps_file} #end if
--comparison ${cond.comparis.comparison} #if $cond.comparis.comparison == 'onevsmany' --control '${cond.comparis.controlgroup}' #end if
#if $cond.advanced.choice == 'true': --test ${cond.advanced.test} --pValue ${cond.advanced.pValue} --adjusted ${cond.advanced.adjusted} --fChange ${cond.advanced.fChange} --generate_svg ${cond.advanced.generateSvg} --generate_pdf ${cond.advanced.generatePdf} --upRegCol '#${cond.advanced.upRegCol}' --downRegCol '#${cond.advanced.downRegCol}' #else --test 'ks' --pValue 0.05 --adjusted false --fChange 1.2 --generate_svg false --generate_pdf true --upRegCol '#ecac68' --downRegCol '#6495ed' #end if
#elif $cond.type_selector == 'user_pvalues': #if $cond.using_ras_user.check == 'true': --user_pvalues_ras ${cond.using_ras_user.user_pvalues_ras_file} #end if #if $cond.using_rps_user.check == 'true': --user_pvalues_rps ${cond.using_rps_user.user_pvalues_rps_file} #end if --generate_svg ${cond.generateSvg} --generate_pdf ${cond.generatePdf} --pValue ${cond.pValue} --fChange ${cond.fChange} --test 'ks' --adjusted false --net false --upRegCol '#ecac68' --downRegCol '#6495ed'
#end if
--choice_map '${cond_choice_map.choice_map}' #if $cond_choice_map.choice_map == 'Custom': --custom_map ${cond_choice_map.custom_map} #end if

param.name: type_selector
param.label: Input format:
param.option: RAS of group 1 + RAS of group 2 + ... + RAS of group N
RAS of all samples + sample group specification

param.name: input_name
param.label: Dataset's name:
param.help: Default: Dataset

param.name: input_name_rps
param.label: Dataset's name:
param.help: Default: Dataset

param.name: check
param.label: Use net RPS for arrow tip coloring?
param.help: If checked, upload the Net RPS file produced by the Expression2RPS tool. Arrow tips of reversible reactions will be colored using the net RPS values.

param.name: net_rps_file
param.label: Net RPS dataset
param.help: Tab-separated file with net RPS values per reaction and sample, as produced by the Expression2RPS tool (Net RPS output). Reaction IDs must end with _RV.

param.name: controlgroup
param.label: Control group label:
param.help: Name of group label to be compared to others

param.name: choice
param.label: Use advanced options?
param.help: Use this options to choose custom parameters for evaluation: statistical test, pValue, Fold-Change threshold, how to solve (A and NaN) and specify output maps.

param.name: test
param.label: Statistical test to use:
param.help: Select the statistical test to use for the analysis based on your data and the amount of replicates. Paired T-test and Wilcoxon need the same number of replicates in each group.
param.option: DESeq2-like statistical test (requires at least 2 replicates per sample, to use ONLY with RAS)

param.name: pValue
param.label: P-value threshold:
param.help: min value 0

param.name: adjusted
param.label: Use adjusted p-values
param.help: Should the program adjust the P-values based on the FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg) correction?

param.name: fChange
param.label: Fold-Change threshold:
param.help: min value 1

param.name: generateSvg
param.label: Generate SVG map
param.help: should the program generate an editable svg map of the processes?

param.name: generatePdf
param.label: Generate PDF map
param.help: should the program return a non editable (but displayble) pdf map of the processes?

param.name: upRegCol
param.label: Up-regulated color
param.help: Color for up-regulated reactions

param.name: downRegCol
param.label: Down-regulated color
param.help: Color for down-regulated reactions

param.name: check
param.label: Use net RPS for arrow tip coloring?
param.help: If checked, upload the Net RPS file produced by the Expression2RPS tool. Arrow tips of reversible reactions will be colored using the net RPS values.

param.name: net_rps_file
param.label: Net RPS dataset
param.help: Tab-separated file with net RPS values per reaction and sample, as produced by the Expression2RPS tool (Net RPS output). Reaction IDs must end with _RV.

param.name: controlgroup
param.label: Control group label:
param.help: Name of group label to be compared to others

param.name: choice
param.label: Use advanced options?
param.help: Use this options to choose custom parameters for evaluation: statistical test, pValue, Fold-Change threshold, how to solve (A and NaN) and specify output maps.

param.name: test
param.label: Statistical test to use:
param.help: Select the statistical test to use for the analysis based on your data and the amount of replicates. Paired T-test and Wilcoxon need the same number of replicates in each group.
param.option: DESeq2-like statistical test (requires at least 2 replicates per sample, to use ONLY with RAS)

param.name: pValue
param.label: P-value threshold:
param.help: min value 0

param.name: adjusted
param.label: Use adjusted p-values
param.help: Should the program adjust the P-values based on the FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg) correction?

param.name: fChange
param.label: Fold-Change threshold:
param.help: min value 1

param.name: generateSvg
param.label: Generate SVG map
param.help: should the program generate an editable svg map of the processes?

param.name: generatePdf
param.label: Generate PDF map
param.help: should the program return a non editable (but displayble) pdf map of the processes?

param.name: upRegCol
param.label: Up-regulated color
param.help: Color for up-regulated reactions

param.name: downRegCol
param.label: Down-regulated color
param.help: Color for down-regulated reactions

param.name: user_pvalues_ras_file
param.label: Pre-computed RAS enrichment file
param.help: TSV file with columns: ids, P_Value, fold change, z-score, average_1, average_2

param.name: user_pvalues_rps_file
param.label: Pre-computed RPS enrichment file
param.help: TSV file with columns: ids, P_Value, fold change, z-score, average_1, average_2

param.name: pValue
param.label: P-value threshold:
param.help: Reactions with P-value above this threshold will be shown as dashed grey arrows.

param.name: fChange
param.label: Fold-change threshold (direct filter):
param.help: Applied directly to the 'fold change' column in your file.

param.name: generateSvg
param.label: Generate SVG map
param.help: Should the program generate an editable SVG map?

param.name: generatePdf
param.label: Generate PDF map
param.help: Should the program return a non editable PDF map?

tool.help:
Overview -------------
This tool analyzes and visualizes differences in the Reaction Activity Scores (RASs) of groups of samples, as computed by the Expression2RAS tool, of groups of samples.
Accepted files are: - option 1) two or more RAS datasets, each referring to samples in a given group. The user can specify a label for each group (as e.g. "classA" and "classB"); - option 2) one RAS dataset and one group-file specifying the group each sample belongs to; - option 3) one or two pre-computed enrichment files (RAS and/or RPS), already containing p-values, fold changes, z-scores and averages for each reaction.
RAS datasets format: tab-separated text files, reporting the RAS value of each reaction (row) for a given sample (column).
Column header: sample ID. Row header: reaction ID.
Optional files: - custom svg map. Graphical elements must have the same IDs of reactions. See HmrCore svg map for an example. - Net RPS file (from Expression2RPS tool): if provided, arrow tips of reversible reactions will be colored using the net RPS values instead of being computed internally.
The tool generates: - 1) a tab-separated file: reporting fold-change and p-values of reaction activity scores (RASs) between a pair of conditions/classes; - 2) a metabolic map file (downloadable as .svg): visualizing up- and down-regulated reactions between a pair of conditions/classes; - 3) a log file (.txt).
Output options: To calculate P-Values and Fold-Changes and to enrich maps, comparisons are performed for each possible pair of groups (default option 'One vs One').
Alternative options are: - comparison of each group vs. the rest of samples (option 'One vs Rest') - comparison of each group vs. a control group (option 'One vs Control). If this option is selected the user must indicate the control group label.
Output files will be named as classA_vs_classB. Reactions will conventionally be reported as up-regulated (down-regulated) if they are significantly more (less) active in class having label "classA".
Example input -------------
"RAS of group 1 + RAS of group 2 + ... + RAS of group N" option:
RAS Dataset 1:
+------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | Reaction ID| TCGAA62670 | TCGAA62671 | TCGAA62672 | +============+================+================+================+ | r1642 | 0.523167 | 0.371355 | 0.925661 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1643 | 0.568765 | 0.765567 | 0.456789 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1640 | 0.876545 | 0.768933 | 0.987654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1641 | 0.456788 | 0.876543 | 0.876542 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1646 | 0.876543 | 0.786543 | 0.897654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+
RAS Dataset 2:
+------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | Reaction ID| TCGAA62670 | TCGAA62671 | TCGAA62672 | +============+================+================+================+ | r1642 | 0.523167 | 0.371355 | 0.925661 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1643 | 0.568765 | 0.765567 | 0.456789 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1640 | 0.876545 | 0.768933 | 0.987654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1641 | 0.456788 | 0.876543 | 0.876542 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1646 | 0.876543 | 0.786543 | 0.897654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+
"RAS of all samples + sample group specification" option:
RAS Dataset:
+------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | Reaction ID| TCGAA62670 | TCGAA62671 | TCGAA62672 | +============+================+================+================+ | r1642 | 0.523167 | 0.371355 | 0.925661 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1643 | 0.568765 | 0.765567 | 0.456789 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1640 | 0.876545 | 0.768933 | 0.987654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1641 | 0.456788 | 0.876543 | 0.876542 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1646 | 0.876543 | 0.786543 | 0.897654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+
Group-file
+---------------+-----------+ | Patient ID | Class | +===============+===========+ | TCGAAA3529 | MSI | +---------------+-----------+ | TCGAA62671 | MSS | +---------------+-----------+ | TCGAA62672 | MSI | +---------------+-----------+
Advanced options ----------------
Statistical test: the statistical test to use for the analysis based on your data and the amount of replicates. The Kolmogorov-Smirnov test is the default option, but you can choose between T-test (paired), T-test (unpaired), Wilcoxon and Mann-Whitney tests. P-Value threshold: the threshold used for significance in the selected test, to verify whether the distributions of RASs over the samples in two sets are significantly different. Fold-Change threshold: threshold of the fold-change between the average RAS of two groups. Among the reactions that pass the p-value test, only fold-change values larger than the indicated threshold will be visualized on the output metabolic map.
Pre-computed enrichment file ---------------------------- When uploading one or both pre-computed enrichment files the tool skips all dataset loading and statistical computation entirely. The values in the file are used directly to style the metabolic map.
**The only controls available to the user are the two filtering thresholds:**
- **P-value threshold**: reactions with a P-value above this threshold are shown as dashed grey arrows (not significant), regardless of their fold change. The value is applied directly to the ``P_Value`` column in your file.
- **Fold-change threshold**: reactions with ``|fold change|`` below this threshold are shown as solid grey arrows (below threshold). The value is applied directly to the ``fold change`` column in your file.
Both RAS and RPS enrichment files follow the same format. If both are provided, RAS styling is applied to arrow bodies and RPS styling to arrow tips on the same map.
The file must be tab-separated and contain exactly these columns:
+----------+---------+-------------+---------+-----------+-----------+ | ids | P_Value | fold change | z-score | average_1 | average_2 | +==========+=========+=============+=========+===========+===========+ | r1642 | 0.032 | 0.45 | 1.23 | 0.52 | 0.31 | +----------+---------+-------------+---------+-----------+-----------+ | r1643 | 0.071 | -0.21 | -0.87 | 0.41 | 0.53 | +----------+---------+-------------+---------+-----------+-----------+
**ids**: reaction identifier as encoded in the metabolic map (e.g. "GPI", "TALDO1"). Must match the IDs present in the selected map exactly.
**z-score**: z-score of the difference between the two group means, used to scale the width of the arrows on the map. Higher absolute z-scores result in thicker arrows.
**average_1**: mean RAS score of the first group or condition being compared.
**average_2**: mean RAS score of the second group or condition being compared.
.. class:: infomark
**TIP**: If your data is not TAB delimited, use `Convert delimiters to TAB`_.
.. class:: infomark
**TIP**: If the user provide just one dataset for analysis: -Use the Cluster Analysis tool to assign group labels when no prior division information is available -provide an external group file specifying the assignment of each sample, if the group division is known a priori
.. class:: infomark
**TIP**: This tool using the RAS scores computed by Ras generator tool.
@REFERENCE@
.. _Ras tool: http://bimib.disco.unimib.it:5555/?tool_id=toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu%2Frepos%2Fbimib%2Fmarea%2FMaREA+RAS+Generator%2F1.0.6&version=1.0.6&__identifer=auulv6gbp76 .. _Convert delimiters to TAB: http://bimib.disco.unimib.it:5555/?tool_id=Convert+characters1&version=1.0.0&__identifer=76g7trea4j6 .. _MaREA cluster analysis: http://bimib.disco.unimib.it:5555/?tool_id=toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu%2Frepos%2Fbimib%2Fmarea%2FMaREA_cluester%2F1.1.2&version=1.1.2&__identifer=lxbyzn2me9
